Content Handler Functions (XbRC)

The CXBParser defines a set of functions for content handling, the most important of which are startElement, endElement, and characters. Almost all of the parser functionality in XbRC runs through these three functions.

  • startElement
  • endElement
  • characters
  • Other Functions

startElement

virtual HRESULT STDMETHODCALLTYPE startElement( wchar_t __RPC_FAR *pwchNamespaceUri,
                                                int cchNamespaceUri,
                                                wchar_t __RPC_FAR *pwchLocalName,
                                                int cchLocalName,
                                                wchar_t __RPC_FAR *pwchRawName,
                                                int cchRawName,
                                                ISAXAttributes __RPC_FAR* pAttributes );

This handler is called when each tag start is encountered. It includes the name of the tag and its length as pwchLocalName and cchLocalName, respectively. It also includes a pointer to the attributes found for this tag in pAttributes. The other variables passed in are not used in XbRC.

endElement

virtual HRESULT STDMETHODCALLTYPE endElement( wchar_t __RPC_FAR *pwchNamespaceUri,
                                              int cchNamespaceUri,
                                              wchar_t __RPC_FAR *pwchLocalName,
                                              int cchLocalName,
                                              wchar_t __RPC_FAR *pwchRawName,
                                              int cchRawName );

This handler is called when each tag end is encountered. It includes the name of the tag and its length as pwchLocalName and cchLocalName, respectively. The other variables passed in are not used in XbRC.

characters

virtual HRESULT STDMETHODCALLTYPE characters( wchar_t __RPC_FAR *pwchChars,
                                              int cchChars );

This handler is called when tag character data is found. The data is included in the pwchChars variable and its size is set in the cchChars variable. The MSXML SAX parser is free to break up the character data into as many calls to this handler as it cares to.

Other Functions

The remaining content handler functions are included here for completeness. Their implementations in the CXBParser class are very simple or, in some cases, just return after taking no action.

  • startDocument
  • endDocument
  • putDocumentLocator
  • startPrefixMapping
  • endPrefixMapping
  • ignorableWhitespace
  • processingInstruction
  • skippedEntity

startDocument

virtual HRESULT STDMETHODCALLTYPE startDocument();

This handler is called when SAX starts parsing the document. It is called before any parsing is done.

endDocument

virtual HRESULT STDMETHODCALLTYPE endDocument();

This handler is called when SAX finishes parsing the document. No more parsing will be done after this call.

putDocumentLocator

virtual HRESULT STDMETHODCALLTYPE 
                         putDocumentLocator( ISAXLocator __RPC_FAR *pLocator );

This handler saves the current locator. The locator is used to get the current location in the XDX file. It tells you the current line number being parsed, and it tells you the current file being parsed.

startPrefixMapping

virtual HRESULT STDMETHODCALLTYPE startPrefixMapping( wchar_t __RPC_FAR *pwchPrefix,
                                                      int cchPrefix,
                                                      wchar_t __RPC_FAR *pwchUri,
                                                      int cchUri );

This handler is called when a prefix is found to an XML tag that cannot be resolved as an XML namespace prefix. It is not used in XbRC.

endPrefixMapping

virtual HRESULT STDMETHODCALLTYPE endPrefixMapping( wchar_t __RPC_FAR *pwchPrefix,
                                                    int cchPrefix );

This handler is called after the endElement call if the element has a prefix that cannot be resolved as an XML namespace prefix. It is not used in XbRC.

ignorableWhitespace

virtual HRESULT STDMETHODCALLTYPE ignorableWhitespace( wchar_t __RPC_FAR *pwchChars,
                                                       int cchChars );

This handler is not called in the current implementation of MSXML SAX.

processingInstruction

virtual HRESULT STDMETHODCALLTYPE processingInstruction( wchar_t __RPC_FAR *pwchTarget,
                                                         int cchTarget,
                                                         wchar_t __RPC_FAR *pwchData,
                                                         int cchData );

This handler is called when XML processing instructions are found. It is not used in XbRC.

skippedEntity

virtual HRESULT STDMETHODCALLTYPE skippedEntity( wchar_t __RPC_FAR *pwchName,
                                                 int cchName );

This handler is called when an entity has not been seen in a declaration. It is not used in XbRC.
